name: staff@maggiehassan.com
subject: Did you see Pres. Trump's speech?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
Team -- We're on day 18 of President Trump's government shutdown, and his address to the nation tonight did little to quell our concerns.
About 800,000 federal workers' livelihoods have been affected, government entities like the TSA and the USDA are going without funding, mortgage applications are being delayed, and Secret Service employees are going without pay.
This shutdown is having a direct impact on hardworking Americans, and it's time for the Senate to follow the House's lead and pass measures to reopen the government. [**Will you please sign our petition calling on President Trump and Mitch McConnell to end the shutdown and get furloughed Americans back to work?**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fc17e/2a380d31/358a841c/f1715f/883759055/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "https://act.maggiehassan.com/page/s/2019_JanuaryShutdown?source=e20190108_Shutdown2_na&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264")
Senator Hassan stands ready to work with her colleagues on both sides of the aisle to end this recklessness, but she needs to know that you have her back. Thanks for taking action.
-- All of us on Team Maggie

body:
Next Tuesday, Governor Eric Holcomb will hit the airwaves throughout Indiana to deliver his third State of the State address. And we'd like to... | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/indiana/youre-invited-gov-holcombs-state-of-the-state?e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| |
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=6c8aecde37&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| Next Tuesday, Governor Eric Holcomb will hit the airwaves throughout Indiana to deliver his third State of the State Address. He'll talk about the momentum we've built together throughout the state, and the work ahead of us to continue taking Indiana to the Next Level.

**And we'd like to personally invite you to join us at a party to watch Governor Holcomb give the big speech!**

We've got watch parties planned in all corners of the state -- Evansville, Indianapolis, South Bend, Crawfordsville and Charlestown -- with potentially even more in the works.

We'll be bringing your fellow Republicans together to watch the State of the State address, and talk about the real results that Governor Holcomb is working to deliver this legislative session --_from passing Indiana's eighth-straight balanced budget to modernizing our tax code, providing more funding for K-12 education, expanding our successful workforce programs and so much more. _

Find a State of the State Watch Party near you:
**Indianapolis**
Indiana Republican Party HQ
6:30 p.m. ET
[Learn More & RSVP](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=96f0da8125&e=1acd9bb3d6)

**Evansville**
Vanderburgh County Republican HQ
5:30 p.m. CT
[Learn More & RSVP](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=b4219cd698&e=1acd9bb3d6)

**Charlestown**
Charlestown Family Activities Park
6:30 p.m. ET
[Learn More & RSVP](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=44347b7009&e=1acd9bb3d6)
No party near you? We'd love to help you host a party in your community. Contact Matt Organ at [morgan@indiana.gop](mailto:morgan@indiana.gop?subject=Watch%20Party), and he'll be in touch to help you get something organized.

**RSVP or reach out today, and make sure to talk about the big speech on social media using the hashtags #INSOTS and #NextLevel.** We'll see you next Tuesday as we come together to watch Governor Holcomb's State of the State address!

| | | Friends,

We're writing with a quick reminder.

**We can't wait to celebrate with you tomorrow (January 10) at the One Minnesota Inaugural Celebration in Duluth with our new Governor Tim Walz and Lieutenant Governor Peggy Flanagan.**

The celebration is free and open to the public, but tickets are required.** **[If you haven't already, c](https://walzflanagan.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=aa2b1c817521919c572975274&id=f5e7b5a372&e=b70d12d84a)[lick here to reserve your free ticket.](https://walzflanagan.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=aa2b1c817521919c572975274&id=c0456df2b2&e=b70d12d84a)

This special evening will feature light refreshments and music by The Crunchy Bunch.
**Duluth One Minnesota Inaugural Celebration**

**Thursday, January 10, 2019**
5:00 pm - 7:30 pm

**Kirby Student Center Lounge**
University of Minnesota Duluth
1120 Kirby Dr
Duluth, MN 55812
Please email any questions to events@walz2019.org.

[If you haven't already, reserve your ticket right now!](https://walzflanagan.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=aa2b1c817521919c572975274&id=04fb051441&e=b70d12d84a)

Hope to see you tomorrow!

name: Mike Thompson
subject: bipartisan solutions
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mike Thompson For Congress |
body:
Peter,

Yesterday, on the 8th anniversary of former Congresswoman Gabby
Giffords’ shooting, my House colleagues and I took a major step
towards protecting our communities against endemic gun violence
that has claimed too many lives.

We have rolled out a plan to require universal background checks on
all gun sales and hope we can count on your support. Will you answer
the call to action and join us in standing up for gun violence prevention?
( https://act.myngp.com/forms/-1794309593276872192 )

SIGN ON → ( https://act.myngp.com/forms/-1794309593276872192 )

Democrats took back the House with a promise to voters that
we will no longer tolerate inaction on gun violence that has
devastated our communities. We are in a position to push for real
change, and we’re getting right to work.

We need 5,000 signatures by midnight tomorrow to send a clear
message that the American people want a sensible, bipartisan
solution to end gun violence.

Will you add your name right away?
( https://act.myngp.com/forms/-1794309593276872192 )

Thanks for taking action,

Mike

Mike Thompson is a veteran and a longtime public servant. He
represents California's 5th district in Congress, where he fights
for our healthcare and a clean environment every day. He cannot
continue to do his crucial work without the support of people
like you. Consider making a donation of any amount now.

body:
| | Think. Pray. Act. at the Intersection of Faith & Politics.
---
[ ](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66iTtMewf2DL0D-tj3igeTtq6D6VQnfOeiGWooFLyJPGBqK_unlfAQaSOLVdTv8ynfo2nAtLr8Ip-gVQN4ZuseO7c1WKDbankmrz_EuE7cLVaj&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190109intersect01&n=1)
| **In this week's Intersect, read about:**
* _Trump Urges Wall Funding to Fix Border Crisis_
* _Pennsylvania High School’s ‘Bible Ban’_
* _GOP Sen. Cruz, Rep. Rooney Introduce Constitutional Amendment to Impose Term Limits on Congress_
* _House Democrats Seek to Restore Abortion Group Funding_

* * *
# Trump Urges Wall Funding to Fix Border Crisis
President Donald Trump made a televised plea for border wall funding Tuesday night, seeking an edge in the shutdown battle with congressional Democrats as he declared there is “a humanitarian crisis, a crisis of the heart and a crisis of the soul.” Addressing the nation from the Oval Office for the first time, Trump argued for funding on security and humanitarian grounds as he sought to put pressure on newly empowered Democrats amid an extended partial government shutdown. Trump called on Democrats to return to the White House to meet with him, saying it was “immoral” for “politicians to do nothing.” _[read more](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=zveC7zroragI2qrXZZ9NavuIi-j5YeFpVemXbc8akyUs41AG2oxp6s2p8k5_qUaWGU92LC-RI7wtC8q-XSo3-w&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190109intersect01&n=2)_ **THINK.**
If you missed President Trump’s live address yesterday, take a few minutes to read the [full transcript of Trump’s speech](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=3nQtyGU6AIRwl-oCWnvkk8kBjHcTrGrGpOAMD-ayqsJqxS7iz-AdNZNVXocD0JKGrecXlGas5XYXnI7Bq5uMj3fSJ4_-0V-OB6xESQKpyII&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190109intersect01&n=3) to the nation from the Oval Office as well as Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer and House Speaker Nancy Pelosi’s [response](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=3nQtyGU6AIRwl-oCWnvkkzZNQl4eXoUl3fC56Ck3WGQzA0LR_G9krnX9FmPlKyK8DX4hcpZC98G4DI2qMT8ywAVOUSDJb80vjIm9NnuWl-g&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190109intersect01&n=4). President Trump called on Democrats in Congress to provide $5.7 billion to fund the border wall. He explained a physical barrier was necessary to stop illegal drugs and criminals from crossing the border. Trump said, “In the last two years, ICE officers made 266,000 arrests of aliens with criminal records including those charged or convicted of 100,000 assaults, 30,000 sex crimes, and 4,000 violent killings. Over the years thousands of Americans have been brutally killed by those who illegally entered our country, and thousands more lives will be lost if we don’t act right now. This is a humanitarian crisis, a crisis of the heart and a crisis of the soul.” In response, Speaker Pelosi said, “Sadly, much of what we have heard from President Trump throughout this senseless shutdown has been full of misinformation and even malice.” And Minority Leader Schumer said, “There is an obvious solution: separate the shutdown from the arguments over border security.” It’s fair to say that many – not all – Trump critics, and many – not all – Trump defenders, are more interested in scoring political points than doing the right thing. As Christians, we can agree that Christians can advocate for strong border security and the right of a nation to enforce its laws, while still demanding our government treat people in a way that respects the sanctity and dignity of the human person. **PRAY.
** Pray for our leaders in Congress to find common ground to solve both the government shutdown and the security crisis at the border. Pray they would put any pride, or efforts for political gain aside and instead act in the best interest of the American people. **ACT.
** Do you agree with President Trump that it is immoral for our politicians to do nothing in regards to border security? [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66iTtMewf2DL0D-tj3igeTtq6D6VQnfOeiGWooFLyJPGBq3aOiB50_Yk1YoK6AvCMy1U1NWxkUsbp5IcMTvr-5x9xrDAc6Q0WgUV528kJLCZpz2y0ruRNUXQ-BBnRBYn1BcA&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190109intersect01&n=5) [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66iTtMewf2DL0D-tj3igeTtq6D6VQnfOeiGWooFLyJPGBq3aOiB50_Yk1YoK6AvCMy1U1NWxkUsbp5IcMTvr-5x9xrDAc6Q0WgUV528kJLCZpzuSNQor2Sg9uAJeulCgCvIg&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190109intersect01&n=6)
* * *
# Pennsylvania High School’s ‘Bible Ban’
A group of Christian students at a Pennsylvania high school said they are fighting for their right to free speech after being told they cannot hand out Bibles to their friends during lunchtime. In November, the Bible club at Mechanicsburg Area Senior High School was denied a request to pass out Bibles at school. The club said the school has until Monday to rescind the ban. "We're hoping not to sue," said Jeremy Samek, senior counsel for the Independence Law Center, which is representing the students. Samek said the school's ban is unconstitutional. This isn't the first time the Bible club has clashed with Davis Harris, the school's principal. FOX 43 reports this is the third time in the last two years the club has asked the law center for help to resolve problems at the school. Samek called it a "concerning" pattern. The Bible club said it followed school policy and submitted a written request to hand out Bibles. The school responded that it could not do so during school hours. _[read more](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=KVYzWTFUuJ0SsyVZLAg-7N0aW74Q9Pr4JT41AS2U7q3pdAd7uJuLbwCjZrkb71EkBiwNVSny3fiblZXTQuQqZ-FXRARHmO2yhhnCbr3l9KrEFvmqF7wwTlDcjrE_FwV5&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190109intersect01&n=7)_ **THINK.**
The First Amendment of the U.S. Constitution says clearly, “Congress shall make no law respecting an establishment of religion, or prohibiting the free exercise thereof...” But we see the lie of “separation of church and state” (a phrase not found in the U.S. Constitution) widely misunderstood. It’s a phrase taken out of context from Thomas Jefferson’s letter to the Danbury Baptist Association. Jefferson was responding to the Danbury Baptists concern that some might interpret religious expression to be a government given right instead of a God-given inalienable right. And they feared that the government would attempt to regulate their religious freedom. Jefferson responded by assuring them the government would not mandate a state-wide religion and would in no way interfere with religious expression saying the First Amendment builds a [“wall of separation between the church and state.”](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6-Ot0R522QNnS5RD-kMSzV7EKgCHCt97r7fLt3CKHTyt2ojHu4IJm_86b2zl4KlvDliQjl3yR-qUJ4Bqin2beA&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190109intersect01&n=8) Americans have sadly twisted the original intention of Jefferson’s words. The founders greatly supported religion in the public square. In fact, the first textbook ever printed in America, The New England Primer, used Bible lessons to help teach children how to read. This was a textbook many founding fathers used in public school classrooms to teach their children. It’s evident that Americans’ view on religious expression in schools has changed drastically since our nation’s founding. **PRAY.
** Pray for the students, parents, and attorneys who are standing up for their right to have Bibles at school and share it with others. And pray for the principle of Mechanicsburg Area Senior High School to have a change of heart in his decision. **ACT.
** We encourage you to check out a great organization called [Gateways to Better Education](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=IDM9ER6y5_UuKUyx7zMXwIFFk_ccvUMI4YXmi4MZcB4&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190109intersect01&n=9). They equip public school educators, leaders, parents and students to protect and promote religious and academic freedom regarding the Bible and Christianity in the classroom.
* * *
# GOP Sen. Cruz, Rep. Rooney Introduce Constitutional Amendment to Impose Term Limits on Congress
Republican Sen. Ted Cruz and Republican Rep. Francis Rooney proposed a Constitutional amendment that would impose term limits on members of both houses of Congress. The amendment, co-sponsored by Sens. Marco Rubio (R-Fla.), Mike Lee (R-Utah), and David Perdue (R-Ga.), would restrict senators to two six-year terms and House members to three two-year terms. A similar amendment was proposed by Cruz in January of 2017. "For too long, members of Congress have abused their power and ignored the will of the American people," Cruz said. "Term limits on members of Congress offer a solution to the brokenness we see in Washington, D.C. It is long past time for Congress to hold itself accountable. I urge my colleagues to submit this constitutional amendment to the states for speedy ratification." _[read more](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=2Mgt8e8nZa4Qaq2n2uIHTHyqs9l5h12D3Ca8ZeUHKqfvd5s13h5QMGSK8VLbYr5FiypHPTe1SFdI9M0GMaL2S0QSBsiefkOcorE_RzKQ28vuf-1EPXo2fZXm-MjmRxfI&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190109intersect01&n=10)_ **THINK.**
Both Republicans and Democrats have supported the idea of term limits at times. Democratic Representative Beto O’Rourke called for term limits on Congress when he ran against Senator Ted Cruz in Texas in the 2018 elections. And President Trump has also said he supports term limits. Yet despite some political support, this Constitutional Amendment will have a long journey if it is to be added to our U.S. Constitution. There are two ways the Constitutional Amendment of term limits could be enacted. The first is with a two-thirds majority vote in both the House and the Senate. This is the method Senator Cruz and Representative Rooney are pursuing with their introduced legislation. But there is also a second option, where two-thirds of state legislatures (34 states) could pass bills applying for the Term Limits Convention. This would force Congress to call the Convention. The states would then send delegates who would propose term limit amendments. Lastly, three-quarters of the states (38 states) would have to ratify the amendment. **PRAY.
** Let’s continue to pray for our elected leaders that they would govern wisely and justly as long as they are in public office whether they have been there for one term or for decades (Proverbs 29:2). **ACT.
** If you are interested in taking action to enact term limits check out the organization [U.S. Term Limits](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=Opot6Pt2yLv3aaumnH9zA6Vfr9kJhPT9o7yT1N1580hO4oZETRrJp0ZmODlIHyVQ&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190109intersect01&n=11) where you can track the term limits convention progress map and see where your state falls on this issue.
* * *
# House Democrats Seek to Restore Abortion Group Funding
The newly sworn-in Democrat-led House of Representatives voted Thursday on a spending bill to end the federal government shutdown that included a provision to provide funding to Planned Parenthood and other abortion groups. The House voted 241 to 190 for the bill with all Democrats voting for it and all but 7 Republicans voting against it. Pro-life groups and prominent conservatives are speaking out against Speaker of the House Nancy Pelosi and House Democrats for slipping language into the Consolidated Appropriations Act of 2019 that would, among other things, restore federal funding to overseas organizations that provide and promote abortion. Such funding was stripped from organizations like International Planned Parenthood by one of President Donald Trump’s first acts as president when he expanded what was known as the Mexico City Policy preventing foreign aid dollars from being used to fund organizations that perform abortions overseas. Trump’s policy is called Protecting Life in Global Health Assistance and forces NGOs to agree as a condition of receiving federal funding that they will not “perform or actively promote abortion as a method of family planning.” _[read more](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=HTIUaYWGgtW-Sq8jttULzX61HryB4MfdillBHImjj_n2zeI2Js3JaeDJ7lhkqsps7jrdpxI_5qjzrgBqgwLzbdj_8xi2dPruPn9c0LC_Ca7DQ5hsI-AvGWXNz-HVblOwQ56l8Asf9qBM7qSUIo0NYG7iSiGjACc3pzKwAWULQ0c&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190109intersect01&n=12)_ **THINK.**
We are only about one week into a new Congress and already the debate is raging on the issue of the sanctity of life. President Trump put in place some very important protections for the unborn, and if we do not keep a careful watch on Congress, then these advancements could be undone. I’m reminded of Ephesians 6:12, “For our struggle is not against flesh and blood, but against the rulers, against the authorities, against the powers of this dark world and against the spiritual forces of evil in the heavenly realms.” There is a spiritual battle between life and death and as followers of Christ we must do everything we can to uphold human life. It’s important to remember the battle is not just in the United States \- it’s a global struggle. The Mexico City Policy funded abortions all over the world. And thanks to President Trump’s new policy, Protecting Life in Global Health Assistance, Americans are no longer funding abortions overseas. **PRAY.
** Pray today not only for those considering abortion in the U.S. that they would choose life, but pray especially for all those women abroad who may be considering abortion because they have no other options or support. Pray for God to meet every one of their needs and for lives to be protected despite difficult circumstances. **ACT.
** Abortion persists because of ignorance, apathy, and confusion. [Abort73](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=HLmOFePXeozEpOHd8Bs6lQObqgpxPzoJCleK9GrwiK8&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190109intersect01&n=13) is an organization working to change that by highlighting the truth about abortion and providing global statistics and facts. They offer simple ways for you to learn and share with others the facts about abortion!
* * *
_If receiving the Intersect is a blessing to you,[please consider making a donation to My Faith Votes](https://www3-myfaithvotes.nationbuilder.com/donate?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190109intersect01&n=14) so that we can continue to encourage more Christians to think, pray and act!_ [](https://www3-myfaithvotes.nationbuilder.com/donate?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190109intersect01&n=15)

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: Add your name to tell Mitch McConnell to hold a vote to end the government shutdown. Federal workers must get paid and the government must reopen.
body:
[1]Bernie Sanders

Sisters and Brothers -

Last night Trump said that this country faces a national emergency – and
he’s right. But it’s an emergency and a crisis that he created.

We are now on day 19 of Trump's government shutdown with more than 800,000
federal workers still without pay — many of whom are extremely worried
about how they’re going to pay their mortgages, feed their families,
afford their medicine and pay their car loans.

Our federal employees deserve to be treated with respect, not held hostage
as political pawns.

Let me be very clear: The Democratic-led House passed a bill to end the
shutdown — the same bill, I would remind you, that passed the Senate
unanimously just a few weeks ago.

Now it’s time to bring it up for a vote in the Senate. So today I am
calling on Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell to allow the House bill
to come to the floor to get a vote.

But the only way that will happen is if all of us make our voices heard.
So I am asking you today:

[ https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/mcconnell-shutdown?source=em190109-full&t=1001&akid=419%2E150750%2E2DggbV ]Add your name to tell Mitch McConnell to hold a vote to end the
government shutdown. Federal workers must get paid and the government must
reopen.

We received stories from some federal employees who shared how this
shutdown is impacting them. I'd like to take a moment to share a few of
them with you today.

One Smithsonian employee writes: "I live paycheck to paycheck like
nearly all of my co-workers and I am scared that with losing weeks of
income and my health care benefits, one family medical emergency for
myself, my children, or my grandchildren during Donald Trump's shutdown
could make us homeless."

Another worker writes: "I am a federal contractor who is not working and
will never get back pay. Border security is important, but so is making
sure my children eat. Donald Trump does not speak for me or the many
thousands of others who just want to do the work we were hired to do."

And another federal employee writes: "I'm a federal government employee,
a single mom, and homeowner, and my hot water heater just went out
today. I have no idea when our next paycheck will come. This shutdown
needs to end now."

But it’s not just federal employees that are going to be hurt because of
this shutdown. Millions of Americans, including many of our most
vulnerable citizens, will be impacted as well.

If the shutdown continues into February, millions of low-income and
working families who depend on food stamps from the Department of
Agriculture will struggle to put food on the table.

Pregnant mothers and their babies may go without the nutrition assistance
they need to stay healthy as the WIC program is on the verge of running
out of money.

Security at airports and the length of time needed to go through security
could very well be impacted if TSA employees are not getting paid.

Farmers and small businesses won't be able to receive financial assistance
from the Department of Agriculture that they need to survive.

If the federal housing administration is unable to process applications
for potential homeowners, many Americans will not be able to get the homes
they need.

As all of you know, this country faces enormous problems. At a time when
the very wealthy are getting richer, we have millions of workers who are
struggling.

If Trump wants to talk about crises, why don’t we look at the massive
levels of income and wealth inequality. Or the 30 million Americans who
have no health insurance and millions more who cannot afford the medicine
they need. Or the fact that too many seniors are living in poverty, too
many kids can’t afford to go to college, and over 40 million are faced
with outrageous amounts of student debt.

We don’t need to create artificial crises like this government shutdown.
We have enough real ones.

There are enormous issues that must be dealt with. That's what the Senate
and the House must start doing – but first, we need to end this shutdown.

[ https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/mcconnell-shutdown?source=em190109-full&t=1002&akid=419%2E150750%2E2DggbV ]It's time for Mitch McConnell to bring the House bill to a vote so we
can end this shutdown once and for all. Add your name if you agree.

This government shutdown is hurting American families. Thanks for adding
your name to demand that we bring an end to it immediately.

In solidarity,

Bernie Sanders

[ https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/mcconnell-shutdown?source=em190109-full&t=1003&akid=419%2E150750%2E2DggbV ]ADD YOUR NAME

One Smithsonian employee writes: "I live paycheck to paycheck like nearly all of my co-workers and I am scared that with losing weeks of income and my health care benefits, one family medical emergency for myself, my children, or my grandchildren during Donald Trump's shutdown could make us homeless." Another worker writes: "I am a federal contractor who is not working and will never get back pay. Border security is important, but so is making sure my children eat. Donald Trump does not speak for me or the many thousands of others who just want to do the work we were hired to do." And another federal employee writes: "I'm a federal government employee, a single mom, and homeowner, and my hot water heater just went out today. I have no idea when our next paycheck will come. This shutdown needs to end now." But it’s not just federal employees that are going to be hurt because of this shutdown. Millions of Americans, including many of our most vulnerable citizens, will be impacted as well. If the shutdown continues into February, millions of low-income and working families who depend on food stamps from the Department of Agriculture will struggle to put food on the table. Pregnant mothers and their babies may go without the nutrition assistance they need to stay healthy as the WIC program is on the verge of running out of money. Security at airports and the length of time needed to go through security could very well be impacted if TSA employees are not getting paid. Farmers and small businesses won't be able to receive financial assistance from the Department of Agriculture that they need to survive. If the federal housing administration is unable to process applications for potential homeowners, many Americans will not be able to get the homes they need. As all of you know, this country faces enormous problems. At a time when the very wealthy are getting richer, we have millions of workers who are struggling. If Trump wants to talk about crises, why don’t we look at the massive levels of income and wealth inequality. Or the 30 million Americans who have no health insurance and millions more who cannot afford the medicine they need. Or the fact that too many seniors are living in poverty, too many kids can’t afford to go to college, and over 40 million are faced with outrageous amounts of student debt. We don’t need to create artificial crises like this government shutdown. We have enough real ones. There are enormous issues that must be dealt with. That's what the Senate and the House must start doing – but first, we need to end this shutdown. [ ]It's time for Mitch McConnell to bring the House bill to a vote so we can end this shutdown once and for all. Add your name if you agree. This government shutdown is hurting American families. Thanks for adding your name to demand that we bring an end to it immediately. In solidarity, Bernie Sanders [ ]ADD YOUR NAME You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:

subject: President Trump's shutdown is a broken promise to all North Carolinians

body:

[]()

| | | Team,

Today, I sent a letter on behalf of all North Carolinians to President Trump urging him to end his government shutdown.

North Carolina continues to rebuild from hurricane flood damage and implement new changes to prevent future damage from natural disasters. When President Trump visited our state after Hurricane Florence, he promised 100% of the federal government's support. With his shutdown, that promise is nearly impossible to keep.

Our efforts to rebuild and strengthen North Carolina stall every day that federal funds are delayed.

[_**That 's why I'm coming to you and asking you to co-sign my letter to President Trump and urge him to end the shutdown now. Please, click here to add your name today.**_](http://act.roycooper. "https://act.roycooper.com/page/s/co-sign-trump-end-shutdown-now?source=010919&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Rocky Point&state=NC&zip=28457&phone=&occupation=&employer=")

Without the support of the federal government to rebuild after hurricane damage, the future of our communities is at stake.

Our farmers can't get the help they need from the Department of Agriculture after many farms were devastated in the hurricane. And with planting season just around the corner, their livelihoods are at stake.

Entire cities, communities, and homes were destroyed by hurricane flood waters, yet without the support for HUD, we can't strengthen our infrastructure, protect property, and keep citizens out of harm's way of the next disaster.

The spirit of North Carolinians is strong, and our determination to rebuild better than ever has not wavered, but businesses, agriculture, and lives are at risk without the support from the federal government.

[_**With your help, we can send a powerful message to President Trump that the shutdown must end so we can rebuild. Add your name as a co-signer to my letter today.**_](http://act.roycooper. "https://act.roycooper.com/page/s/co-sign-trump-end-shutdown-now?source=010919&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Rocky Point&state=NC&zip=28457&phone=&occupation=&employer=")

Thanks,

Roy
